title: 10 medicinal constituents of Ulmus rubra (slippery elm)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Ulmus rubra triterpenoids is a class of bioactive compounds isolated from the bark and inner bark of the plant. These triterpenoids have been found to exhibit various biological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties. They have also shown potential in inhibiting cancer cell growth and inducing apoptosis, making them a promising area of research for the development of novel therapeutic agents.

Ulmus rubra flavonoids is a group of plant compounds that have been found to possess significant antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, have been identified in the inner bark of the slippery elm tree, which has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for centuries. Research suggests that these flavonoids may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, potentially providing benefits for cardiovascular and respiratory health.

Ulmus rubra phenolic acids is a type of compound found in its bark and inner tissue. These acidic molecules are composed of hydroxyl groups attached to a central ring structure. They have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to the plant's traditional uses in herbal medicine for soothing digestive issues and wounds.

Ulmus rubra quercetin is a bioactive compound extracted from the inner bark of the red elm tree. This flavonoid has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for centuries to treat various health issues, including inflammation and respiratory problems. Quercetin's anti-inflammatory properties are attributed to its ability to inhibit the release of histamine and other chemical mediators involved in allergic reactions, making it a popular natural remedy for allergies and asthma.

Ulmus rubra kaempferol is a flavonoid compound isolated from its bark. This bioactive compound has been shown to possess various pharmacological properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Kaempferol has been reported to exhibit potential health benefits, such as protecting against oxidative stress, modulating immune responses, and inhibiting cancer cell proliferation. Its chemical structure is characterized by a flavonoid skeleton with a 2-phenylchromen-4-one framework.

Ulmus rubra gallic acid is a type of phenolic compound found in the bark of this native North American tree. Gallic acid is responsible for many of slippery elm's medicinal properties, including its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. It has also been shown to possess antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it a valuable ingredient in traditional herbal remedies.

Ulmus rubra ellagic acid is a powerful polyphenol antioxidant found in its inner bark. This compound has been extensively studied for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and anticancer properties. Ellagic acid has been shown to inhibit the growth of cancer cells, induce apoptosis (cell death), and protect against oxidative stress. Additionally, it may help reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health by inhibiting platelet aggregation and cholesterol synthesis.

Ulmus rubra catechins is a type of polyphenol antioxidant found in its inner bark. Catechins are known for their high antioxidant activity and ability to protect the body against free radicals. Ulmus rubra catechins have been studied for their potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce the risk of chronic diseases.

Ulmus rubra aldehydes is a type of natural compound extracted from the bark of the plant. These aldehydes have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them potentially useful in treating various health conditions such as skin irritations and digestive disorders. The aldehydes have also been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity, inhibiting the growth of certain bacteria and fungi.

Ulmus rubra lignans is a type of polyphenolic compound found in the bark of the plant. These compounds have been shown to possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Lignans from Ulmus rubra have also been traditionally used in herbal remedies for their potential benefits in wound healing and digestive health.

intro: Ulmus rubra has active constituents such as mucilage, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These constituents impart medicinal properties that aid in soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. The mucilage, in particular, forms a protective barrier in the gut, coating and calming irritated tissues, while flavonoids and phenolic acids exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. This plant's properties can improve people's lives by alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), ulcers, and colitis, promoting digestive health and comfort.
intro_desc: Ulmus rubra has active constituents such as mucilages, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These constituents confer medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and soothing effects. Mucilages, in particular, help to form a protective barrier on mucous membranes, providing relief from irritable bowel syndrome and other gastrointestinal issues. This can lead to improved digestion and reduced discomfort for those suffering from these conditions.
